name: T1490 - vssadmin Shadow Copy Deletion
description: Detects vssadmin.exe invoked with destructive shadow-copy arguments. Used by ransomware families including LockBit, Conti, BlackCat/ALPHV, Ryuk, and Hive to eliminate Volume Shadow Copies before or after file encryption, preventing victims from restoring files without paying ransom.
query: process where process.name : "vssadmin.exe" and process.args : ("delete", "resize") and process.args : ("shadows", "shadowstorage")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: T1490 - bcdedit Boot Recovery Disabled
description: Detects bcdedit.exe commands that disable Windows Recovery Environment (recoveryenabled No) or suppress boot failures (bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures). Ransomware uses these to prevent the operating system from entering repair or recovery mode after encryption, maximising pressure on victims to pay.
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: T1490 - wbadmin Backup Catalog Destroyed
description: Detects wbadmin.exe deleting backup catalogs or system-state backups. Ransomware destroys Windows Backup data to eliminate offline restore options that would allow victims to recover without paying. Particularly prevalent in enterprise-targeting groups such as Conti and Royal.
query: process where process.name : "wbadmin.exe" and process.command_line : ("*delete catalog*", "*delete systemstatebackup*", "*keepVersions:0*", "*delete backup*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: T1490 - WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
description: Detects wmic.exe deleting Win32_ShadowCopy instances. Ransomware variants use WMIC as an alternative to vssadmin.exe to evade process-name-based detections while achieving the same recovery-inhibition outcome.
query: process where process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : "*shadowcopy*" and process.command_line : ("*delete*", "*call delete*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: T1490 - Recovery Inhibition Multi-Tool Sequence (Pre-Encryption Signal)
description: Sequence detection — flags a host where a recovery-tool process (vssadmin, bcdedit, wbadmin) is followed by a second distinct recovery-tool process within 3 minutes on the same host. This multi-tool co-occurrence is a high-fidelity pre-encryption signal present in virtually all major ransomware families including LockBit, Conti, BlackCat/ALPHV, Ryuk, Hive, Royal, Play, and BlackBasta.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=3m
  [process where process.name : ("vssadmin.exe", "bcdedit.exe", "wbadmin.exe", "wmic.exe")]
  [process where process.name : ("vssadmin.exe", "bcdedit.exe", "wbadmin.exe", "wmic.exe") and not process.name : _[0].process.name]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
